dialogueChunks.intro: I hate to be the bearer of more bad news, {player_name}. But the misfortune isn't done with us yet.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The remedy you gathered? It's not working. The alchemist says the blight has mutated into something they've never seen before, and the standard treatment only slows it. {target_npc} has been pouring over old texts and found a reference to a stronger compound that requires {quest_item} from deep caves {location_hint}. The caves are dangerous: unstable tunnels, pockets of foul air, and {enemy_type} nesting in the dark. But without the stronger compound, the blight wins.
dialogueChunks.outro: Every time we find a solution, the problem changes shape. But we can't stop trying.

playerResponses.text: Then I'll go deeper. Whatever it takes.
A mutating blight? This job keeps getting harder. And more expensive.
I can't chase a disease that keeps evolving. I'm sorry.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the universe has a terrible sense of humor. Listen to this.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A rockslide blocked the passage you cleared to the new vein. The very next day. {target_npc} checked it and says it'll take weeks to dig through with the tools we have. But there might be another way in: an old drainage tunnel {location_hint} that the original miners used. It's been sealed and forgotten, probably because the {enemy_type} took it over when the mine was abandoned. We need you to clear it out and bring back enough {quest_item} to keep the excavation going while we dig through the main blockage.
dialogueChunks.outro: One step forward, two steps back. That's been the rhythm of our lives lately.

playerResponses.text: A sealed tunnel full of monsters? Just another day at this point. I'll go.
Your luck is genuinely astounding. Fine, show me the drainage tunnel.
Even the mountain is fighting you. I think this mine is done.

dialogueChunks.intro: It's getting worse, {player_name}. I didn't think that was possible, but here we are.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The sickness spread to the alchemist. The one person who knows how to prepare the cure is now too ill to stand. {target_npc} found the alchemist's notes, but they're written in shorthand that nobody else can read. There's another alchemist who might help: a recluse living {location_hint} who was expelled from {location} years ago over a dispute. We need you to find them, convince them to read the notes, and bring back enough {quest_item} for a proper batch of medicine. The recluse's territory is swarming with {enemy_type}, which is probably why they chose it.
dialogueChunks.outro: Our healer is sick, our notes are unreadable, and our last hope is a hermit who hates us. Misfortune doesn't even begin to cover it.

playerResponses.text: I'll find the recluse and get the notes translated. We're not giving up.
A bitter hermit in monster territory? This quest has everything.
Diplomacy with angry hermits isn't really my skill set.

dialogueChunks.intro: Fate has a new twist for us, {player_name}. This one might be the cruelest yet.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The alternate route you scouted? It worked for three days. Then the {enemy_type} returned in numbers we've never seen: driven out of their territory by something even they're afraid of. They've swarmed every path between {location} and the outside world. {target_npc} managed to get a message to a trader on the far side, and the trader left a cache of {quest_item} hidden {location_hint}. But reaching it means fighting through the densest concentration of {enemy_type} we've ever encountered.
dialogueChunks.outro: The supplies are out there, tantalizingly close. But the gauntlet between us and them is the worst it's ever been.

playerResponses.text: I've fought through worse. Tell me where the cache is.
A gauntlet of monsters? At least this time there's a clear objective at the end.
Whatever scared the {enemy_type} out of their territory scares me more.
